thinking.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture of the idea. Let me analyze what needs to be created:

**The Core Idea**: A feedback/comment system for the sdlc workspace that allows collaborative annotation on living artifacts (ponder entries, features). The current `feedback.yaml` is a global capture queue with no context, no identity, no persistence lifecycle.

**Two possible interpretations from the ponder sessions:**
1. **Comments on ponder slugs** (design-decisions.md) — add a `comments.yaml` to existing ponder entries
2. **FeedbackThread as a standalone primitive** (thread-primitive.md) — a new lightweight primitive at `.sdlc/threads/<slug>/`


The scrapbook reveals both approaches were tested across the sessions. The design decisions doc pushed toward comments as a first-class feature on ponder slugs, while the thread-primitive doc outlined a standalone FeedbackThread with a locked V1 scope covering create, comment, and show operations. An HTML prototype was even built for the UI. There's a real tension here between embedding comments directly on ponder entries versus treating threads as their own independent primitive, and I need to figure out which direction the milestones actually support.

Looking at the current milestone structure, `feedback-polish` is active with two features focused on editing and enrichment, but there's nothing capturing a full thread system yet. I'm thinking this warrants a new milestone for the thread primitive itself, with three main pieces: the data model and CLI operations, a REST API layer, and the UI components for browsing and viewing threads. Alternatively, if we go the ponder-comments route, it'd be the same three-layer approach but anchored to ponder entries instead. I should check what `feedback-polish` already covers to make sure I'm not duplicating work.

input.command: sdlc feature list --milestone feedback-polish 2>&1 || sdlc milestone info feedback-polish 2>&1
sdlc feature show feedback-edit 2>&1 && echo "---" && sdlc feature show feedback-enrich 2>&1
input.description: Check what feedback features already cover

thinking.thinking:
Good. So `feedback-polish` is about editing and enriching existing feedback notes — still in the old paradigm (feedback.yaml global queue). This ponder is proposing a fundamentally different architecture: contextual, identified, persistent comment threads on ponder entries.

This is clearly a new direction, not an extension of what's in `feedback-polish`. The new feature needs its own milestone.

**Scope analysis:**
Looking at the ponder artifacts:
- Two models explored: comments on ponder slugs, and standalone FeedbackThread 
- The sessions show V1 scope is locked around Thread primitive
- Design prototype exists (HTML mockup)


- The design work converged on FeedbackThread as a standalone primitive across multiple sessions, with a UI prototype already built out

I'll go with FeedbackThread as the primary primitive for the new milestone since that's where the design thinking landed. **New milestone**: `v20-feedback-threads`

Now I'm mapping out the V1 scope based on what was locked in the thread-primitive docs: core functionality covers creating threads, adding comments, and viewing them through both CLI and REST API, plus the UI layer for browsing and reading threads. Synthesis and promoting threads to ponder slugs can wait for V2. This breaks down into three features: the data model and API layer, the UI components, and then synthesis as a future phase. That feels like the right scope for a single milestone. `feedback-thread-ui` — UI: sidebar nav, thread list, thread detail (body + comment thread + compose)

V2 can wait for synthesis and promotion to ponder. That gives me two solid V1 features. Let me set up a milestone called `v20-feedback-threads` since `v19-uat-artifacts` is the current highest.

text: Now I have a clear picture. Let me synthesize this into milestones and features.

input.command: sdlc milestone create v20-feedback-threads --title "FeedbackThread — persistent, contextual, collaborative idea threads" --vision "Replace the global feedback queue with a lightweight thread primitive: every idea gets a live comment thread that humans and agents both read and write" 2>&1
sdlc milestone create v20-feedback-threads --title "FeedbackThread — persistent, contextual, collaborative idea threads" --version v20 2>&1
input.description: Create v20 milestone with correct args

input.command: sdlc milestone create v20-feedback-threads --title "FeedbackThread — persistent, contextual, collaborative idea threads" 2>&1

sdlc feature create feedback-thread-core --title "FeedbackThread core: data model, CLI, and REST API" 2>&1 && sdlc feature create feedback-thread-ui --title "FeedbackThread UI: thread list, detail view, and sidebar nav" 2>&1
sdlc milestone add-feature v20-feedback-threads feedback-thread-core 2>&1 && sdlc milestone add-feature v20-feedback-threads feedback-thread-ui 2>&1

text: Now let me write the vision into the milestone manifest and link the ponder:

input.new_string:

title: FeedbackThread — persistent, contextual, collaborative idea threads
vision: Replace the global feedback queue with a lightweight thread primitive. Every idea gets a live comment thread that humans and agents both read and write — contextual, identified, append-only, and always visible per slug.
features:
- feedback-thread-core
- feedback-thread-ui

text:
Done. Here's what was created:

---

## Summary

**Milestone created:** `v20-feedback-threads`
> FeedbackThread — persistent, contextual, collaborative idea threads

**Vision:** Replace the global feedback queue with a lightweight thread primitive. Every idea gets a live comment thread that humans and agents both read and write — contextual, identified, append-only, and always visible per slug.

**Features (2):**

| Feature | Scope |
|---|---|
| `feedback-thread-core` | FeedbackThread data model (`.sdlc/threads/<slug>/`), CLI (`sdlc thread create/comment/show`), REST API (`/api/threads`) |
| `feedback-thread-ui` | Thread list page, thread detail (body + comment thread + compose box), sidebar nav item |

**V2 deferred (future milestone):** synthesis mechanism (versioned body rewrites) and promote-to-ponder graduation path — both well-designed in the scrapbook but explicitly out of V1 scope per session conclusions.

---

**Next:** `/sdlc-run feedback-thread-core`
